This message appears to be the outbound touchpoint that was sent *to* the lead (Carolyn), it's showing as an inbound message in the system, but the content and signature (", Jon") make clear this is actually the outbound intro email that went out to this contact.

Since this is the outbound message and there's no actual reply from the lead yet, there's nothing to respond to. Staying silent here is the right call.
Something came up on my end and the booking didn't go through. I'll flag this for Luke directly so he can confirm Monday May 4th at 9am with you. He'll reach out shortly.
Monday May 4th at 9am is open. Before I lock it in, what email should I use for the calendar invite?
Friday isn't showing any open slots. Closest I've got is Monday May 4th at 9am or 9:30am. Does either of those work?
Hey Jon, did you get that calendar fixed?
Still having trouble with the calendar on my end. I'll flag Friday at 1pm directly with Luke so he can confirm with you. You'll hear from him shortly.
Having trouble pulling up the calendar right now. I'll flag this for Luke directly so he can confirm Friday with you. Does before 8am or around 1pm work better if you had to pick one?
Can you get me on the calendar with Luke?

Can't pull listings directly, but Luke can filter for basements when he pulls options for you.

On neighborhoods, that's really a Luke question. He knows Ada County well and can point you toward the right fit.

Want to get a quick call on the books?
Can't pull listings directly, but Luke can filter by bedrooms, price, and basement when he pulls options for you. Want to get a quick call on the books?

HOA fees vary a lot depending on the neighborhood. Newer developments and planned communities tend to run $50-$200/month. Some areas have no HOA at all. Luke can tell you exactly what applies to any specific property you're looking at.

On tours, what day and time work best for you next week?

Don't have a current price-per-sqft number I'd trust right now, that shifts frequently and I'd rather not guess.

On 4-bed homes under 600k in Ada County, yes they exist but that range moves fast. Luke can pull what's actually available right now and filter by your criteria. Want to get a call on the books?

On property taxes, already covered that above. Ada County runs 0.6% to 0.8% of assessed value, so roughly $3, 600 to $4, 800 a year on a $600k home.

For tours, Luke is the one to set those up and he'll know exactly what's worth seeing at your budget. Want me to get you two connected?
I'd love to schedule a tour next week if possible.

For neighborhood recommendations, that's really a Luke Gilbert question. He knows Ada County well and can point you toward the right fit based on what you're looking for.

On inventory, it's been shifting. Last we talked, Ada County was sitting around 1, 459 active listings. For 4-bed homes under 600k specifically, those tend to move faster than the overall market.

Want to get a call on the books with Luke?

Ada County property taxes generally run around 0.6% to 0.8% of assessed value. On a $550k home that's roughly $3, 300 to $4, 400 a year, though it varies by location and exemptions.

For neighborhood recommendations, that's more of a Luke Gilbert question. He knows the area well and can point you toward the right fit. Want me to get a call on the books?

On property taxes, Ada County generally runs around 0.6% to 0.8% of assessed value. On a $550k home that's roughly $3, 300 to $4, 400 a year, but it varies by exact location and exemptions. Luke can give you the real breakdown for specific areas.

Want to get a call on the books?

I can't pull specific listings, but Luke can walk you through exactly what's active in your range and set up tours.

What day and time work best for a quick call with him?

On closing costs, buyers typically budget 2-5% of the purchase price. At 750k that's roughly $15k-$37k covering lender fees, title, escrow, and prepaids like insurance and taxes.

On the tour, I can't schedule showings directly, but Luke can set that up for you. What day and time work best for a quick call with him?
